title: Senecillis retusa
intro: Senecio retusa, also known as Groundsel or Cineraria, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Senecio retusa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Senecio retusa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical preparations to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Senecillis retusa is classified in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. It belongs to the genus Senecio, which consists of a large number of species, mostly native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Senecio is further divided into several subgenera, with Senecio s. str. being one of them. Senecillis retusa is also known to be a synonym of some species in the genus Senecio, which can cause some confusion in classification. The species' exact classification can vary depending on the source and the authority used.
